hero.title: Turn your routine into consistent progress.
hero.description: Training, nutrition, body composition, and metabolism in one coaching system with memory, context, and practical guidance for everyday life.

showcase.title: The operating system for your progress
showcase.description: Dashboard, chat, and metabolic insight working together to turn your routine into practical clarity.
showcase.chat_example_user: I trained less this week and missed my targets on some days. What makes the most sense today?
showcase.chat_example_trainer: Today's goal is not extreme compensation. Let's protect consistency with a short workout, high protein, and a simple calorie target.

how.title: How it works in practice
how.subtitle: Less guessing. More clarity on what to do today.

steps.title: Your data goes in
steps.desc: Workouts, meals, weight, and body composition are tracked manually or imported.

steps.title: The system organizes signals and trends
steps.desc: FityQ turns scattered routine data into clear context on consistency, balance, and progress.

steps.title: You get practical guidance
steps.desc: Your mentor uses memory and context to respond clearly, not with generic advice.

diff.title: Why FityQ feels different
diff.subtitle: It is not just a chat. It is a system that turns your routine into useful context.

memory.title: Memory that learns from your routine
memory.description: The system remembers what has been happening to answer with better context.

metabolism.title: Adaptive metabolic reading
metabolism.description: Your metabolism stops being a fixed formula and starts reflecting your real data.

dashboard.title: Dashboard with connected signals
dashboard.description: Weight, fat, muscle, calories, activity, and frequency in one place.

trainers.title: Mentors with different styles
trainers.description: The same context can produce different guidance depending on who fits you best.

integrations.title: Real integrations and imports
integrations.description: Use the tools you already track to accelerate the system's usefulness.

multichannel.title: Support beyond the app
multichannel.description: In Pro, you can also use chat images and Telegram access.

trainers.title: Choose the coaching style that fits you
trainers.description: Each mentor reads the same context with a different personality. You choose the one that matches how you like to grow.

atlas.for_who: For people who prefer clarity and data-guided decisions.
atlas.best_for: Training adjustments, progress interpretation, and routine organization.
atlas.example: Your progress improves more with simple consistency than extreme corrections.

luna.for_who: For people seeking consistency with more lightness and balance.
luna.best_for: Sustainable routine, recovery, and reconnecting with habits.
luna.example: Today's best move is lowering pressure and protecting the pace you can sustain.

sofia.for_who: For people who value physiological context and metabolic reading.
sofia.best_for: Metabolism, women's health, and routine adjustments with more context.
sofia.example: Before tightening your diet, we need to understand what this week is actually signaling.

sargento.for_who: For people who respond better to pressure and firm structure.
sargento.example: Your plan does not need to be more complicated. It needs to be executed.

gymbro.for_who: For people who want encouragement, energy, and partnership.
gymbro.style: Motivational, close, and easy to follow.
gymbro.best_for: Consistency, confidence, and routine without extra pressure.
gymbro.example: Let's get back to the basics done well. That's what builds real results.

plans.title: Start simple. Grow with context.
plans.subtitle: The free trial exists so you can feel the system working in your routine before deciding.

basic.name: Basic
basic.description: Your personal trainer for daily use, without automations.

pro.name: Pro
pro.description: The full experience with data, photos, and automations.

items.q: Do I need to connect apps for it to work?
items.a: No. You can start with manual tracking and add integrations later.

items.q: Does it work if I do not have a perfect routine?
items.a: Yes. The idea is to help you stay consistent in real life, not demand perfection.
FityQ uses your routine history to respond with more context and continuity over time.
The metabolic reading uses your weight and nutrition data to produce an adaptive estimate instead of relying only on fixed formulas.

items.q: What is the difference between plans?
items.a: The free trial lets you experience the core proposal. Paid plans expand usage volume, mentor access, and resources like images, integrations, and Telegram.
Yes. You can stop your subscription without long-term contracts.

items.q: Does FityQ replace medical or nutritional guidance?
items.a: No. The product provides digital support for routine, habits, and performance, but does not replace individualized professional care.

items.q: Where can I read about privacy and terms of use?
items.a: Links to Privacy Policy and Terms of Use are available on the landing page and in the footer.

cta.title: Start building a smarter routine
cta.subtitle: Try FityQ and see how your routine can turn into clarity, context, and consistency.

demo.title: See how FityQ works in everyday life
demo.subtitle: Your routine creates signals. FityQ organizes that context and turns it into practical guidance you can actually follow.
demo.step_1_title: Workout, meals, and weight enter the system
demo.step_1_description: You track the basics manually or import data you already use.
demo.step_2_description: FityQ crosses frequency, intake, weight, and recovery to detect what changed.
demo.step_3_title: You get the next step with clarity
demo.step_3_description: Instead of generic motivation, you get useful guidance for that moment.

demo_case.label: Realistic example
demo_case.title: A normal week, handled with more intelligence
context_points: Weight fluctuating without a clear trend
demo_case.trainer_reply: You do not need to overcompensate. Your best move today is to restore consistency: short workout, high protein, and a simple calorie target.

sofia.user_1: Did I build a good plan for this week?
sofia.trainer_1: Yes. I adjusted volume, rest, and protein distribution using your recent recovery data.
sofia.user_2: What should I prioritize today to hit the goal?
sofia.trainer_2: Protein at lunch, carbs before training, and a lighter dinner to close the day.

gymbro.user_1: My chest workout felt flat today.
gymbro.trainer_1: Then we raise intensity without losing technique. Less ego, better execution.
gymbro.user_2: Should we finish with a short cardio block?
gymbro.trainer_2: Yes. Fifteen focused minutes already help recovery and the deficit.

integrations_section.title: Bring your routine into the system
integrations_section.subtitle: The more context goes in, the more useful the guidance becomes.
integrations_section.hevy_desc: Import or sync workouts to turn execution into context.
integrations_section.mfp_desc: Bring nutrition history to connect intake and progress.
integrations_section.zepp_desc: Use weight and body composition data to enrich the system reading.
